# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 134

This is turn 134. Turns 1 through 133 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 134.

## Turn 134 — the \(h_0\)-block passes the complete quadratic gate

Turn 133 retired the old \(A_2\) word and proposed the following marked repair. 

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation remains implicit, as required by the submission specification. 

### Universal class-two calculation

Let \(E\) be either a mixed Heisenberg coefficient group or an extraspecial quadratic coefficient group. Thus
\[
Z(E)\simeq C_2,\qquad E/Z(E)\ \text{is elementary abelian},
\]
so \(E\) has class at most \(2\), every commutator has order at most \(2\), and every element has fourth power \(1\).

Fix a lower tuple and write \(X,D\in E\) for the coefficient components of \(x_0,d_0\). Let \(\varphi\) be the automorphism of \(E\) induced by conjugation with \(g_0\). Since all lower values occurring in the \(h_0\)-block lie in the wild kernel, they act trivially on the simple coefficient module. Consequently the coefficient component of \(h_0\) is
\[
H=\varphi(X)\,X\,\varphi(D)\,D\,D^2
   \bigl(\varphi(D)^{-1}D^{-1}\varphi(D)D\bigr).
\]

Put \(a=\varphi(D)\), \(b=D\), and
\[
k=a^{-1}b^{-1}ab.
\]
In \(E\), \(k\) is central and \(k^2=1\). Since \(ab=bak\),
\[
ab\,b^2k=ba\,b^2=b^3a=b^{-1}a.
\]
Hence the exact class-two identity is
\[
\boxed{H=\varphi(X)\,X\,D^{-1}\varphi(D).}
\tag{134.1}
\]

This already includes all possible central second-order components of \(d_0\) and all variation coming from the conjugator \(g_0\); it is stronger than a formal Hessian calculation.

### The two projector branches

Recall
\[
Dd_0=Pb+(P+1)c,\qquad \varphi|_{E/Z}=U^{-2},
\]
where \(P\) is the tame-inertia norm projector and \(U=S^{\omega_2}\).

#### Branch \(P=0\)

Here \(D\) and \(X\) have the same image in \(E/Z(E)\). Thus \(D=ZX\) for some \(Z\in Z(E)\). Since \(\varphi\) fixes the central involution,
\[
D^{-1}\varphi(D)=X^{-1}\varphi(X).
\]
Equation (134.1) gives
\[
H=\varphi(X)XX^{-1}\varphi(X)=\varphi(X)^2=X^2.
\tag{134.2}
\]

#### Branch \(P=1\)

Here inertia is trivial and \(U=1\) on every simple characteristic-two module. Conjugation by \(\sigma_2\) therefore induces the identity on \(E/Z(E)\). The kernel of
\[
\operatorname{Aut}(E)\longrightarrow\operatorname{Aut}(E/Z(E))
\]
is an elementary abelian \(2\)-group of central automorphisms. Hence its square is trivial. Since
\[
g_0=\sigma_2^2,
\]
conjugation by \(g_0\) is the identity on \(E\). Therefore (134.1) again gives
\[
H=X^2.
\tag{134.3}
\]

Thus, in both branches,
\[
\boxed{\text{the complete class-two value of }h_0\text{ is exactly }x_0^2.}
\tag{134.4}
\]

### Consequences

This settles both kinds of second-order data simultaneously.

For the mixed Heisenberg probe, \(h_0\) contributes exactly the old \(x_0^2\) coefficient:
\[
(c,\lambda)\longmapsto \lambda(c).
\]

For an extraspecial extension attached to an invariant quadratic form \(q\), it contributes exactly
\[
c\longmapsto q(c).
\]

Moreover, the removed factor \(x_1^4\) is identically trivial in every class-two exponent-four probe. Therefore the **entire new wild word**, not merely its \(h_0\)-subword, has the same simple-module first and second derivatives as the old \(A_2\) word.

In particular, the previously derived formulas survive unchanged:
\[
\langle c,\lambda\rangle_{\rm new}
=
\begin{cases}
\lambda(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
\lambda\!\left((1+U+U^{-1})c\right),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\]
and
\[
Q_{\rm new}(c)
=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0.
\end{cases}
\]

So the coefficient-duality operator remains invertible, and the candidate-side determinant/Arf form remains minus type in the unramified branch and hyperbolic in the ramified branch, subject to the already-derived local comparison.

There is also a useful scalar check. In the corrected pro-\(2\) specialization,
\[
h_0u_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma c_0
=
x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma].
\]
If \(A,B,C\) are the mod-\(2\) Magnus variables for \(\sigma,x_0,x_1\), its degree-two initial form is still
\[
B^2+AC+CA.
\]
Thus the repair changes the essential \(\mathbf Z_2\)-marking while leaving the mod-\(2\) Hilbert/Bockstein form unchanged.

The \(h_0\)-trial should therefore be **retained**. This is not yet a presentation claim, but the mixed-Heisenberg Hessian and extraspecial quadratic gate are passed uniformly, not case by case.

[Turn 134 quadratic certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn134_h0_quadratic_certificate.py)

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved an exact class-two identity showing that the \(h_0\)-block evaluates as \(x_0^2\) in both projector branches. Consequently the repaired word has exactly the old mixed-Heisenberg Hessian and extraspecial quadratic form, while its pro-\(2\) specialization has the corrected unramified marking.

**GAP**: Higher-order marked tame–wild gluing remains untested. In particular, the quadratic calculation cannot detect the new \(\sigma^2\)-marking, whose first genuinely new terms occur beyond degree two.

**NEXT**: Compute the degree-three marked jet and turn the proposed \(C_5\rtimes C_4\) and \(C_{17}\rtimes C_8\) checks into one uniform family for tame quotients with \(\operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m\), coupled to characteristic quotients of the corrected marked Demuškin group.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
